title: pilosella setosopetiolata
intro: Pilosella setosopetiolata, also known as the hairy-fruited mouse-ear hawkweed, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it beneficial for wound healing and reducing swelling. The plant's medicinal properties also include antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can help to boost the immune system and protect against infections. Pilosella setosopetiolata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its health benefits, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
